{"id":"project-LICHEN-gwfz","title":"Lollipop version transition from circular to linear may cause spurious rejoin","description":"File: lichen/subsys/lichen/rpl/dodag.c:36-59

The lollipop version comparison handles linear (0-127) and circular (128-255) regions separately, but the transition between them has a subtle edge case.

When a node is in circular region (e.g., version=250) and receives a linear version (e.g., version=0), the code at line 58 returns `a < LOLLIPOP_CIRCULAR_BIT` (true), indicating the linear version is 'newer'.

Per RFC 6550 Section 7.2, this is the intended restart behavior - a node restarting from version 0 should be accepted. However, this also means:

1. An attacker with knowledge of current version could send a DIO with version=0 to force all nodes to rejoin
2. Legitimate out-of-order delivery of old DIOs (from before a restart) would be incorrectly treated as newer

Impact: Potential DODAG instability or denial-of-service via version manipulation.

Note: The SECURITY comment in routing.c mentions link-layer auth, but this version attack could come from any authenticated neighbor that has been compromised or is replaying old messages.

Mitigation: Consider adding a cooldown period after restart, or logging suspicious version transitions.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-12T06:54:40.664506Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-12T06:54:40.664531Z"}

{"id":"project-LICHEN-gwfz","title":"Lollipop version transition from circular to linear may cause spurious rejoin","description":"File: lichen/subsys/lichen/rpl/dodag.c:36-59

The lollipop version comparison handles linear (0-127) and circular (128-255) regions separately, but the transition between them has a subtle edge case.

When a node is in circular region (e.g., version=250) and receives a linear version (e.g., version=0), the code at line 58 returns `a < LOLLIPOP_CIRCULAR_BIT` (true), indicating the linear version is 'newer'.

Per RFC 6550 Section 7.2, this is the intended restart behavior - a node restarting from version 0 should be accepted. However, this also means:

1. An attacker with knowledge of current version could send a DIO with version=0 to force all nodes to rejoin
2. Legitimate out-of-order delivery of old DIOs (from before a restart) would be incorrectly treated as newer

Impact: Potential DODAG instability or denial-of-service via version manipulation.

Note: The SECURITY comment in routing.c mentions link-layer auth, but this version attack could come from any authenticated neighbor that has been compromised or is replaying old messages.

Mitigation: Consider adding a cooldown period after restart, or logging suspicious version transitions.","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"AI Agent","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-12T06:54:40.664506Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-22T22:52:20.307118573Z","started_at":"2026-07-22T22:52:20.307118573Z","lease_expires_at":"2026-07-22T22:57:20.307118573Z","heartbeat_at":"2026-07-22T22:52:20.307118573Z"}
